summary.intent: Address 6 Codex review findings on the file_upload component in PR #693, applying TDD fixes and syncing the spec.
summary.outcome: 5 of 6 findings were fixed with new tests (all green), clippy clean, 3626 tests passing, spec synced; finding 6 was disputed (spec contradicts Codex's premise) and left unresolved pending user decision, then committed and pushed.

repo: The `ars-components` test suite has 7 pre-existing failures in `data_display::progress` and `data_display::meter` under the default feature set due to i18n percent-formatting; they pass under `--features i18n` (CI profile).
The Service's `context_changed` flag is set on any `plan.apply` closure execution with no PartialEq comparison — firing SetProps always signals a context change.
The Leptos adapter increments `context_version` on `context_changed`, which is how adapters detect state mutations.
The spec's anatomy (§2) places Trigger and Dropzone as siblings under FileUpload, not nested — Codex's finding 6 premise contradicted this.
Spec §1.7 is illustrative pseudo-code referencing functions like `generate_file_id()`, `use_platform_effects()`, `trigger_click_on_element()` that don't match the real API.
Tests construct Context via `Machine::init(&props, ...)`, never by literal struct construction — adding Context fields is safe without updating tests.
Adapters are not yet implemented (core-only PR #301); adapter-level concerns like DOM bubbling belong in future adapter tasks.
`cargo xtask spec validate` checks 340 spec files for conformance; must pass with no snapshot drift after spec edits.

code.finding: `on_props_changed` was not including `capture`/`name` in change detection, and `hidden_input_attrs` read from `props` instead of `ctx`, causing stale hidden input attributes after prop updates.
`SetProps` did not reset DragOver state when the component became disabled/readonly mid-drag, leaving the machine stuck.
`SetFiles` mutated `ctx.files` but never reconciled `state`, leaving machine stuck in Uploading state when a new file set had no uploading items.
`generate_file_id` reused IDs after file removal; fixed by adding a monotonic `next_file_id` counter to Context, seeded from existing files in `init` and bumped on each new ID assignment.
`update_file_progress` updated any file matching `file_id` regardless of status; fixed by guarding on `Status::Uploading`.

workflow: TDD red→green cycle was enforced: new tests were written and confirmed failing (compile errors) before any implementation.
Stashing changes to confirm pre-existing failures is a reliable way to distinguish regressions from baseline failures.
When Codex review findings contradict the spec, the correct resolution is to surface the disagreement to the user rather than implementing a fix that violates the spec contract.
Spec sync is a required contract: every behavioral change to the core must also update the corresponding spec file.
Coverage annotation (`^0` markers) should be checked to verify new branches are exercised, distinguishing new uncovered paths from pre-existing defensive ones.

friction: One new test had a flawed premise: after `SetFiles(Some(...))` makes the Bindable controlled, `RemoveFile` only updates `internal` so `get()` still returns the controlled value — the test had to be redesigned to use the uncontrolled path.
A clippy warning about passing a `Copy` type (`State`) by reference required a two-step fix (signature + call site deref).
The spec's §1.7 is pseudo-code that doesn't compile or match the real API, making it an unreliable reference for implementation decisions.
Line numbers in the spec file shifted after early edits, requiring re-reads before each subsequent spec edit.
open_items: Finding 6 (nested Trigger-in-Dropzone causing double OpenFilePicker dispatch) is unresolved — disputed as contradicting the spec's sibling anatomy; if a future adapter renders Trigger inside Dropzone, the fix belongs in that adapter via `stopPropagation`.
Adapter implementations for file_upload are not yet written (deferred to a post-PR #301 task); DOM-level concerns like click bubbling will need to be revisited there.

The two pre-existing uncovered branch markers (`^0`) in `file_upload/mod.rs` — the `UploadProgress` guard-false case and the `strip_prefix('file-')`-None case — are defensive paths with no tests; low priority but noted.
